behaviors.description: Embedding vectors produced for a given query have the correct dimensionality for the vector database index
behaviors.justification: The real failure occurs when the embedding model produces vectors of wrong dimensionality (e.g., 768-dim vs. 1536-dim expected), which the vector DB rejects or silently mishandles. A Unit test with mocked embedding would always return correct dimensions and hide this failure. Integration level is necessary: call the real embedding API (or accept it exists), validate output dimensionality against actual vector DB schema, and ensure compatibility before any queries execute.
behaviors.test_description: Call the embedding model API with sample product queries, verify each returned vector matches the vector database's expected dimensionality (e.g., exactly 1536 dimensions), and confirm vectors can be inserted into or queried against the actual vector database schema without dimension-mismatch errors
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test properly requires validation against real/test vector DB schema. Mocking would hide real API failures.

behaviors.description: Similarity search results are ordered by descending cosine similarity score
behaviors.justification: The failure occurs when the vector database returns results in arbitrary or ascending order instead of descending similarity. A Unit test with mocked vector DB would always return pre-sorted results and hide the real failure. Integration level is required to test against an actual or test vector database instance, insert vectors with known similarity relationships, and verify strict descending-order enforcement on actual similarity calculations.
behaviors.test_description: Insert 10+ test vectors with varying known similarities into the test vector database, query for a reference vector, retrieve results, and assert that returned results are strictly ordered by descending cosine similarity (first result has highest similarity, last has lowest)
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test properly requires real vector database with actual similarity computations. Mocks would not catch ordering failures.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranking step reorders results when handling queries with nuanced or contextual intent
behaviors.justification: The failure occurs when the LLM fails to understand query nuance (e.g., distinguishing 'budget phone for durability' from 'budget phone for gaming') and returns results in the same order as raw similarity ranking. Mocking the LLM would always return a controlled output, completely hiding whether the real LLM understands semantic intent. Real LLM inference is non-deterministic and context-sensitive; without actual model calls, you cannot verify the system catches nuanced intent. Agentic level is required because correctness depends on real LLM judgment, which is inherently non-deterministic and may vary across runs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute 5+ test queries with nuanced intent (e.g., 'durable phones under $300' vs. 'fastest phones under $300'), record the raw similarity-ranked results from the vector DB, send each query to the real LLM re-ranker, and verify that at least 80% of queries produce a different top-3 result ordering post-reranking that aligns with query semantics
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic test requires real LLM calls to evaluate semantic understanding. Mocked LLM would always pass, failing to catch real semantic misunderstanding.

behaviors.description: Queries with no close semantic matches return an empty result set instead of low-relevance noise
behaviors.justification: The failure occurs when the API lacks or fails to enforce a similarity threshold (e.g., cosine similarity < 0.3), returning low-relevance results. A Unit test with mocked vector DB could hide this if the mock doesn't test threshold enforcement on actual low-similarity scores. Integration level is required to test against a real or test vector database, insert actual product data, construct queries with no high-similarity matches, and verify that either zero results are returned or only high-similarity results (above threshold) are included.
behaviors.test_description: Load test product data into the vector database, construct queries guaranteed to have no matches above a predefined similarity threshold (e.g., < 0.25), execute each query through the API, and assert that the response is either empty or contains only results with similarity scores above the threshold
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test properly requires real/test vector database with actual similarity scores and threshold enforcement logic. Mocks could hide threshold failures.

behaviors.description: End-to-end latency from query receipt to ranked results stays below 2 seconds (median case)
behaviors.justification: The failure occurs when real-world latency (embedding API + vector DB + LLM inference) exceeds 2 seconds. Unit mocks complete in milliseconds, completely hiding real latency. System-level tests (real embedding + vector DB but mocked fast LLM) would miss the dominant latency source: LLM inference, which typically takes 1–5 seconds and is non-deterministic. Agentic level is required because LLM inference latency is both the dominant component and non-deterministic—it varies across runs and depends on model load, input complexity, and sampling strategy. Without real LLM calls, latency tests are useless.
behaviors.test_description: Execute 30+ realistic product queries end-to-end using the real embedding API, vector database, and LLM re-ranker in sequence, measure total latency from query input to ranked results returned, and assert that the 50th percentile (median) latency is below 2 seconds
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic test requires real LLM inference, which is the primary latency contributor and is non-deterministic. System-level test with mocked LLM would not catch actual LLM latency failures.
